hotspots.location: forward sweep loop starting at line 50
hotspots.reason: Iterates over all rows and non-zero elements, performing multiple memory accesses and computations.

hotspots.location: backward sweep loop starting at line 70
hotspots.reason: Similar to the forward sweep, iterates over all rows and non-zero elements, performing multiple memory accesses and computations.
Involves communication overhead in distributed memory environments.

bottleneck_type.reasoning: The primary bottleneck is memory-bound due to frequent memory accesses to matrix values and indices, which dominate the computational cost.

optimization_suggestions.suggestion: Use loop unrolling and blocking techniques
optimization_suggestions.details: Unroll the inner loop to reduce loop overhead and improve cache utilization. Use blocking to enhance data locality.
Use non-blocking communication to overlap data exchange with computation, reducing the effective communication time.

gpu_suitability.reasoning: The problem is suitable for GPU acceleration due to its high degree of parallelism in the matrix operations.
gpu_suitability.recommended_approach: Port the forward and backward sweep loops to GPU using CUDA or OpenCL, focusing on optimizing memory access patterns and using shared memory to reduce global memory accesses.
